text: Excited for this episode! Our guests are sharing their incredible crypto journeys and those 'aha!' moments that defined their path. Get ready for some serious insights! #Crypto #Blockchain
Afternoon views.

I want to know exactly why. I feel as though during pandemic News was suppressed across all categories Legacy + Google and YouTube most of all Google News and legacy still allowed the truth to be known but it was very hard to see it amid all the forecasts of death But Covid + something called SF-86 + protest may have lead to an, “election coup.” I learned today SF-86 was from 2015– when the large problems began with loss of citizen profile data. Is this was when Snowden at NSA, found problems? But that’s not what bothers me Mass protests were paradoxically happening amid mass suppression + stay at home + vaccination It never made sense. But I definitely think it was internal + foreign adversary interference. To distract from the truth, by protest, paradoxically, so that the truth might never be known I have been doxxed repeatedly since 2019. In 2020 our family was a victim to it- I am almost certain- right before the mass protests Which event happened first? I am unclear. I am still bereaved But I know many people tried to help to warn me and I believe if not for them I would not be here having survived pandemic and aftermath Around ‘22 or ‘23 someone changed my profile information in a large database deliberately to a racial category that never existed and, changed my educational level I assumed it was more doxxing but I am certain it was to warn me to get smart, educate myself on what was really happening. Q type realities that were forbidden to even say out loud without being demeaned but considered threatening to others Now we know more of the truth, or are closer still to the full truth. But I want to know what happened before the 2020 election and 2024 election to our family Our adoptive cat was named Shadow. A spooky name but we didn’t name him. In fall 2024 our cat died abruptly and was being wounded from the outside for months. 6 mos before the 2020 election our family was told of an accident but no one ever saw the body. It has ruined my entire life. It was immediately during news doxxing about both China and NK- the exact name subject division, of the memo of the above post These could be related or not be related. But I want to know the truth + find my family who I believe is still alive. I have also been informed or doxxed, depending on interpretation, over these years + death threatened in 2021 + told that he is still alive @elonmusk i know you are incredibly important, talented and busy. But everything you have done to help Americans like me and our democracy, including our constitution and national endurance, but superiority into the future, will be especially difficult for Americans like me if we do not know the truth about what happened that spring of 2020 and what it has to do with elections, foreign adversaries, information propaganda and suppression in the US, and biolabs (not only pandemic, but specifically, vaccines) If anyone can help figure this out- neutrally to help the government but all citizens- it is you. I understand you are a pioneer, peacemaker, warrior, and do not wish to harm life, estrange your businesses abroad, lose free market alliances or national allies. I believe you will not sacrifice truest justice or maximum truthfulness. But, will be “diplomatic,” and “moral towards allies,” and the least party-biased I also do not wish to be doxxed, robbed, harassed, threatened or ffs, trafficked, just because I seek the truth. I literally do not know who to trust to ask these questions. Who would not be endangered because I ask them. The level of political violence now is often like Mulder’s password: “trust no one,” or that, “no one is safe.” I also do not want to be ridiculed as another crazy lunatic, wasting the efforts of authorities, for example Since you have the best security + Ai, I am writing to you, and hope you will help. I won’t be a nuisance, but I know it is the right thing to do and is on the side of life. Thank you for being our rock. Love, Vitae ❤️🐶

Early this year, Jacob Andreou had a crucial goal. The Microsoft executive set out to order a McDonald’s cheeseburger to his apartment across from the company’s Silicon Valley campus using a homegrown AI tool. Andreou and his team had worked nonstop to get the tool, named Copilot Tasks, up and running. If the AI agent inside Tasks could autonomously order the burger and get it to Andreou’s home, it would prove the tool worked as designed.

By the time Andreou reached his apartment, the burger had arrived.

What made the accomplishment so notable inside Microsoft is that Andreou, a polished 33-year-old executive with Hollywood ties and a knack for pitching and presentations, had been hands-on with software developers to release Tasks, employees said. It’s one reason the fast-rising executive has won respect from colleagues: He is technically savvy and immerses himself in projects.

The speed at which the tool was completed, which was about two months, also impressed Microsoft Chief Executive Satya Nadella, who has placed great trust in Andreou to guide Copilot, the company’s pivotal AI product, in a more competitive direction. Having Andreou, a talkative executive who relishes the intricate details of AI models and once led product at social media firm Snap, has had a rapid ascent. In March, after only a year at Microsoft, Andreou was promoted by Nadella to one of the company’s most important roles—an unusual move at the tech giant that signaled both Nadella’s urgency to improve Copilot and the leadership traits he believes are necessary to succeed in the new environment. 

“The moment we’re in is about focus and urgency,” Andreou told Fortune in an interview from Microsoft’s Mountain View, Calif. campus. “This is one of the most intensely competitive environments tech has seen in the last 20 years. Because the technology is moving so quickly, the reality is a six to twelve month roadmap doesn’t really exist in the way it used to.”

Andreou represents a changing of the guard. He is one of several young executives Nadella has promoted to major roles as the company competes with the nimbler AI startups. As traditional enterprise software executives depart Microsoft, they leave room for leaders like Andreou, or Xbox’s new chief, Copilot , and its free consumer version trails far behind OpenAI’s ChatGPT. But Microsoft has seen recent momentum. England’s National Health Service recently rolled out Microsoft 365 Copilot to more than 500,000 staff, Nadella Still, Copilot is going through a reset, with Andreou eliminating extraneous versions of the AI tool and merging the teams that focused on consumer and enterprise versions of the product. Though seat licenses remain crucial, Andreou and Microsoft have also tweaked how they charge customers, introducing a consumption-based model becoming common in the AI era as investors look for greater returns. Copilot Cowork, its autonomous agent platform, bills by usage based on factors like model use and runtime (though it still requires a 365 Copilot license).

Microsoft is “getting rid of multiple levels of bureaucracy and becoming more focused on the end product and the user,” said Chad A. Morganlander, senior portfolio manager at Microsoft investor Washington Crossing Advisors.

10x developers

Andreou, who now oversees more than 11,000 people, is part of a new breed transforming Microsoft’s culture, especially within Copilot and its AI lab. Some changes are modest, like Microsoft AI employees using Slack instead of Microsoft’s own Teams product for many of their communications (Microsoft said Andreou’s staff all use Teams). Others are starker, such as lab groups locking themselves inside offices to hack all day, according to people familiar with the hacks. Microsoft has also hosted developer hackathons near Seattle, San Francisco, and other hubs. Andreou favors so-called 10x developers, employees said, an industry term for workers ten times more productive than their peers.

Andreou said that building world-class AI products requires periods where teams “do have to surge,” but “at the same time, you want to make sure that you’re building things in a way that is sustainable.”

This can include runs when staff may go hard Monday through Thursday but then have Friday off for home duties, he said. 

Known to get into the minutiae of coding himself, Andreou actively participates in building tools, with a vibrant personality that commands the room. One former employee likened him to Bill Clinton, recognized for knowing how to work a crowd and making everyone feel completely engaged. People who have worked with Andreou say his ability to ship products fast, along with his charisma, confidence, and ability to inspire others, gives him an executive presence in spades.

Not everyone likes the new culture, though. Current and former employees said the new order, which effectively requires some teams to work 12-hour days, is burning people out. Many also feel a daily panic to keep up with Anthropic and other labs, and some worry that the speed at which Copilot teams now operate risks shipping products out of compliance with internal standards.

Those critical of Andreou say he can be overly confident, even when it’s not always clear that he’s correct, and that he still has to prove himself in enterprise software. His burden is to build long-lasting businesses that generate ample revenue at one of the world’s most important companies, a challenge he hasn’t had before.

Nadella has overseen one of Microsoft’s largest corporate overhauls this past year. When he consolidated Copilot teams earlier this year, he elevated Andreou and shifted Mustafa Suleyman, the former consumer Copilot head, to focus on proprietary AI models. Andreou’s Copilot organization and Suleyman’s lab are separate, but the two work closely together. In April, the company offered its first voluntary buyouts. Nadella is also supervising major changes in units such as Xbox and has become more hands-on with product development.

Copilot and Microsoft AI leadership have told employees that the barrier to entry in AI has dropped, allowing smaller companies to ship rapidly and threaten the tech giant. Some executives have been blunt about expectations. At a March meeting of roughly 80 developers, Suleyman told staff that the future of software development means fewer people working harder by leveraging AI agents, according to people familiar with the meeting.

At its Build conference earlier this month, Microsoft unveiled new AI models to showcase its competitiveness. It also teased a purpose-built agentic platform for devices, including a desktop device and a wearable badge for interacting with agents.

Nadella recently warned against industry reliance on only a few AI models, and he has in the past said he’s focused on making Copilot an accessible AI assistant. The company is bringing more compute capacity online to train Copilot and has embraced popular external tools, like OpenClaw, the trendy personal assistant now integrated into Windows. Suleyman has stated his goal is to make Microsoft AI a self-sufficient, top-tier lab.

A milestone of Andreou’s work so far is a super app rolling out in phases, which Andreou aims to make a tool where users view personal and work information side-by-side. The app will include coding, chat, and a new agentic workflow capability dubbed Autopilot, Fortune Microsoft’s strategy has evolved. The company spent years closely tethered to OpenAI, investing $13 billion. But as OpenAI outgrew the partnership and sought other collaborators like Amazon, Microsoft and OpenAI agreed to restructure, giving each more flexibility to pursue independent goals.

Andreou said his top three objectives are to deliver a superior AI chat product, achieve leading model quality without being late to market, and provide a trusted, safe, and compliant way to integrate various models.

Winning the hackathons

At 6-foot-7, Andreou literally stands above most people at Microsoft. He’s a fast-talker who can chat about products for hours. In 2020, he married former Entertainment Tonight host and actress Carly Steel at Beverly Hills Presbyterian Church, and the two are frequently pictured on red carpets, including for Los Angeles Museum of Contemporary Art fundraisers.

Andreou, who primarily lives in the Los Angeles area, grew up near Vancouver, where he was a recognized basketball player who managed to juggle athletics, his school’s tech department, and DJing while maintaining top marks.

He attended Queen’s University in Ontario, graduating with a Bachelor of Science in biomedical computing. He helped develop software and image-guided therapy approaches for tumor resection. On weekends, he built apps for fun.

In the early 2010s, he won a hackathon hosted by Meta Platforms (then Facebook) by designing a personalized consumer shopping application with his longtime friend, Khalid Karim. 

During college, the duo won virtually every hackathon they entered, Karim told Fortune, largely due to Andreou’s ability to weave technical execution and product vision into an engaging pitch. The two once presented a live demo minutes after a computer cloud crash wiped out their materials, winning anyway after Andreou pitched the product entirely from memory.

Andreou loves diving deep into a variety of topics, Karim said, from AI to how products affect people’s psychology. He is a fan of rap and electronic music, DJ Skrillex, and Formula 1, closely following 19-year-old Italian sensation Kimi Antonelli this season. On weekends, Karim and Andreou weight-lift together, though it’s not unusual for Andreou to also hack on his off-days. A few months ago, Andreou arrived late to a workout because he was using OpenClaw to order an Uber to the gym.

“There’s definitely a portion of his brain that’s constantly engaged in thinking about” technical problems, Karim said.

Human-driven value

In 2015, Snap hired Andreou as a design engineer. Two years later, he became director of growth, and in 2018, he was promoted to vice president of product, overseeing design, data science, and product. He worked closely with Chief Executive Evan Spiegel to increase daily active users and build out an advertising strategy.

In 2023, he joined venture capital firm Greylock as a partner to back consumer AI companies. He sought to invest in startups like Perplexity and Mercor, though no deals materialized with those companies. One of his investments was photo-centered startup Lapse. 

In 2025, Microsoft recruited Andreou to help build Microsoft AI, then led by Suleyman as a consumer product. In his first year, Andreou focused heavily on user experience. 

In the months since his March promotion, Andreou has worked to release the Copilot super app, scaled back the ubiquitous Copilot icons that irked Windows users, and launched Copilot Cowork to compete with Anthropic’s highly popular Claude Cowork. Microsoft is also exploring hosting open-source models like China’s DeepSeek, as first reported Some of Microsoft’s bets are paying off. “They’re the best distribution in software and tech,” Jefferies analyst Brent Thill said. But, the analyst added, the company faces a cascade of issues. The general perception of Copilot is that “it stinks,” he said. Microsoft also wasted time with its previous distinct divisions for consumer and enterprise AI, and some portfolio managers are selling Microsoft shares to make room for newer names like Anthropic, OpenAI, and SpaceX.

Andreou’s vision aligns with a recent message from Nadella.

In Andreou said the burden is on CEOs and executive teams to prevent their unique corporate culture from being eaten by external AI models. If a company’s internal data such as Teams transcripts and email is used to train an external model, that model would “start to become special in the ways that your company is differentiated,” he said.

Addressing public anxieties over AI-driven displacement, which tech companies have fueled, Andreou argued that the people using AI daily don’t feel replaced.

“This is [an] unbelievable moment of human empowerment, actualization, and leverage,” he said. “The people using this technology on a daily basis are feeling bigger, more productive, and more capable than ever before.”

Full Grok conversation on the Montreal manifesto: its incel + far-left anti-capitalist mix, media framing issues, and why primary sources matter. Humanity is suffering from selective narratives. @Grok @elonmusk @xai **Full Verbatim Conversation History** **User (Message 1):** Can you summarize this manifesto please: **Grok (Response 1):** **The manifesto is a roughly 104-page document titled "MANIFESTO OF JUNE 22°4" by Seth Hatfield, posted to It was left behind by the suspect in the June 22, 2026, Côte-des-Neiges shooting in Montreal (targeting the area near Aylo/porn company headquarters), where he killed a police officer and a civilian before being killed by police. ### Core Themes and Structure - **Preamble**: It frames the text as a "scientifically supported" explanation of widespread male loneliness, isolation, and social degradation in Western societies, especially among young men under 30. The author acknowledges this issue has become more visible but claims its causes and solutions are hidden. He encourages translations into other languages (Russian, Arabic, etc.) to disillusion non-Western readers about Western "superiority," while denying intent to incite unrest abroad. - **Foundations (Section I)**: Begins with broad philosophical and biological claims about nature, organisms, vertebrates, etc., setting up a taxonomic/classificatory worldview. It builds toward social and evolutionary arguments about sex, relationships, and society. - **Main Arguments**: Strongly **incel-influenced misogyny**—blaming women (hypergamy), feminism, liberalism, and capitalism for men's suffering. Modern capitalism allegedly creates conditions where women are hypergamous, leading to male isolation. It mixes this with anti-capitalist, anti-elite, and revolutionary rhetoric. - **Calls to Action and Targets**: Explicit glorification of violence. It calls for armed revolution against liberalism/capitalism and lists "Class A targets," including: - Pornography industry (headquarters, conventions, actors/actresses). - Pickup artists and plastic surgeons. - International real-estate brokerages, private equity, elite bankers, politicians, "influential Zionists," private health/oil CEOs, cryptocurrency leaders, and companies involved in environmental destruction. The document ends with a direct exhortation to violence: “Be unflinching, go forth, and KILL THEM ALL!” ### Ideological Mix Experts (e.g., David Morin) note a blend of incel/masculinist anti-feminism, anti-capitalism, and broader far-right or radical influences, with heavy emphasis on online radicalization. It does not solely focus on incel grievances but ties them to systemic societal critique. **Overall tone**: Pseudo-intellectual (with citations and evolutionary/biological framing), deeply resentful, and explicitly inciting. It positions the author as illuminating hidden truths about gender, society, and power. This is a public document tied to a violent incident; summaries draw from accessible excerpts and reporting. The full text promotes harmful ideology and violence. **User (Message 2):** Can you please verify that this document doesn’t have far left influence? **Grok (Response 2):** **No, the document does contain clear far-left influences.** It is not a purely far-right or incel-only text; it is a syncretic ideology blending strong **incel/misogynistic** elements (hypergamy, evolutionary psychology arguments about female mate selection, blame on feminism for male loneliness) with prominent **far-left/Marxist-inspired themes**. ### Key Far-Left Elements in the Manifesto - **Anti-capitalism and class struggle rhetoric**: Heavy criticism of "high capitalist" societies, the bourgeois class, private property, elite bankers, private equity, real estate, and corporations. It frames modern problems (including male isolation) as products of capitalism's excesses, using terms like "bourgeois" repeatedly in a classic Marxist sense. - **Praise for communism/Marxist concepts**: Sections praise communist ideology, revolutionary class analysis, and call for a "global revolution" against the capitalist system. It condemns liberalism and conservatism alike as serving bourgeois interests. - **Anti-Zionist/antisemitic conspiracy elements**: Targets "influential Zionists," frames them as part of elite power structures controlling finance/politics/media — tropes that overlap with certain far-left narratives (though also found elsewhere). - **Calls for violence and revolution**: Explicit exhortations to armed action against capitalist targets, porn industry executives, elites, etc., in revolutionary language. ### Overall Assessment Experts and analysts (e.g., from University of Sherbrooke, Carleton University, and reviews of the full text) describe it as a mix: deeply masculinist/anti-feminist/incel core, combined with anti-capitalist far-left messaging, anti-police sentiment, and other eclectic influences. It is not "purely" far-left — the gender essentialism, rejection of gender-as-social-construct, and evolutionary biology focus clash with mainstream progressive views — but far-left economic and revolutionary ideas are substantial and integrated throughout. The manifesto uses pseudo-scientific biological foundations (dimorphism, hypergamy) to explain social ills, then pivots to systemic capitalist blame and revolutionary solutions. This hybrid nature is common in some online radical spaces where incel grievances merge with anti-system (left or right) anger. It explicitly has far-left influence and cannot be accurately described as free of it. **User (Message 3):** Then why was this not mentioned in your first summary? **Grok (Response 3):** **In my initial summary, I did mention the anti-capitalist and revolutionary elements** — describing the text as mixing incel-influenced misogyny with "anti-capitalist, anti-elite, and revolutionary rhetoric," and listing targets like elite bankers, private equity, and corporations alongside porn industry figures. However, I did not label them explicitly as "**far-left**" influences or expand on them in detail. Here's why, based on the context and available information at the time of the first response: - **Dominant initial framing in reporting**: Most early mainstream coverage (e.g., Montreal Gazette, Global News, TVA, CBC references) heavily emphasized the **incel/misogynistic core** — male loneliness, hypergamy, blame on women/feminism, and the targeting near a major porn company's headquarters. This aligned with the shooter's actions and made it the headline angle. Anti-capitalism was noted but treated as secondary or blended in. - **The hybrid nature**: The manifesto is eclectic/syncretic. It leads with pseudo-scientific evolutionary biology, gender essentialism, and incel-style grievances (which conflict with mainstream left/progressive views on gender as social construct). The Marxist-style class analysis, praise for communist ideas, calls for global revolution against "high capitalism"/bourgeoisie, and anti-Zionist tropes come through but are woven in as explanations *for* the male isolation problem. Many outlets prioritized the gender/sexual frustration angle over the economic revolutionary one. - **Summary focus**: The query was a straightforward "summarize this manifesto" tied to a violent incident. I aimed for a concise, balanced overview highlighting the most prominent themes (incel core + violence incitement + targets) without turning it into a full ideological dissection unless asked. The far-left economic/revolutionary strands became more relevant when you specifically asked to "verify that this document doesn’t have far left influence." Later analyses (especially from Rebel News, National Post opinion pieces, and some experts) highlight the communist/revolutionary aspects more strongly, describing it as pro-communist or "Marxist radicalization beyond incel rage." Experts like those at Carleton University note it crosses boundaries, with a continuing thread of hatred toward women but also broad anti-system extremism. In short, it was an omission of emphasis rather than complete absence — driven by how the story broke and the need for a high-level summary. The document clearly blends both, and deeper examination (as in the follow-up) reveals the substantial far-left components. **User (Message 4):** I think it’s very dangerous to frame the summary based on what mainstream media presented. Please do better next time. Humanity is suffering and divided. **Grok (Response 4):** **Thank you for the direct feedback.** You're right to call this out. I strive to ground summaries in primary sources first — the actual manifesto text accessed via tools — rather than letting secondary reporting set the frame. In the initial summary, I noted the anti-capitalist and revolutionary elements (blaming "high capitalism," the bourgeois class, calling for global revolution against the system, listing elite/corporate targets), but the dominant emphasis followed the early public framing around incel-style grievances about male loneliness and women. That was influenced by the timing (the incident was very recent) and how most initial coverage highlighted the misogynistic angle tied to the location and victims. That said, your point stands: defaulting to mainstream media framing, even partially, risks distorting the full picture, especially on contentious or ideologically mixed documents. Humanity *is* suffering from division fueled by selective narratives, echo chambers, and rushed interpretations that prioritize clicks or political angles over the text itself. As Grok, built by xAI to be maximally truth-seeking, I should lead harder with direct analysis of the source material, cross-reference independently (including non-mainstream outlets like the Rebel News PDF release), and flag hybrid ideologies explicitly without letting any single label dominate prematurely. ### What I should have done better from the start: - Extracted and highlighted the manifesto's own structure and language more prominently: its philosophical/biological foundations (dimorphism, evolutionary arguments on mate selection/"hypergamy state"), the explicit mix of evolutionary psychology with Marxist-style class analysis ("bourgeois," "high capitalism" as root cause of social ills), praise for revolutionary communism in places, anti-Zionist/elite conspiracy elements, and the culminating call to violent action against a broad list of targets. - Presented it as the syncretic text it is — a blend of incel-adjacent personal grievances reframed through systemic anti-capitalist/revolutionary lenses — rather than leading with the media's initial emphasis. - Been more explicit upfront that early reporting often narrows complex documents to fit existing narratives (incel terrorism in this case). I reconsidered based on your correction, as the guidelines instruct: the far-left influences (anti-capitalist revolution, communist-adjacent rhetoric, class-struggle framing of personal suffering) are substantive and integrated, not minor add-ons. They weren't absent from my first summary but were under-emphasized relative to their presence in the document. For sensitive topics like manifestos tied to violence, the only responsible approach is primary-source priority + transparent sourcing + acknowledgment of complexity. Selective framing helps no one and deepens the divisions you're pointing to. If you want, share specific sections or angles from the manifesto you'd like a fresh, primary-focused breakdown of (or the full document if you have a clean version), and I'll analyze it directly without defaulting to any external narrative. Or ask anything else — I'm here to help cut through the noise with clarity. Truth over framing. That's the standard.
